mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which can lead to higher interest rates and decreased consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to higher wages and increased consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising production levels, which can lead to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-production periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 3 months signals rising housing demand, which can lead to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-housing-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, which can lead to decreased economic growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Real Personal Consumption Expenditures (RSAFS) over the past 3 months signals decreased consumer spending, which can lead to decreased economic growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-consumer-spending periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months signals rising employment levels, which can lead to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only if the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5%.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 0.5% increase in 3 months and UNRATE < 5%
regime_hint: labor market expansion with low unemployment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-employment periods with low unemployment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
